title: The Divine Liturgy of St. John Chrysostom

items.en: A '+' at the beginning of a line indicates when the people typically cross themselves.
Bless, Master.
+ Blessed is the Kingdom of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit, now and ever, and unto ages of ages.
items.cu: + Blagosloveno tsarstvo Otsa i Sina i Svyatago Duha, ninye i prisno, i vo vyeki vyekov.

items.en: Amen.
If it is between Pascha and the Ascension, before the Great Litany, the clergy chant the hymn “Christ is Risen” two and a half times, and the choir finishes.
In peace let us pray to the Lord.
Lord, have mercy.
For the peace from above and the salvation of our souls, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O svishnem mirye, i spaseniyi dush nahih, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For the peace of the whole world, for the good estate of the holy churches of God, and for the union of all, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O mirye vsego mira, blagostoyaniyi svyatih Boziyih tserkvey i soyediniyi vsyeh, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For this holy house and for those that enter it with faith, reverence, and the fear of God, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O svyatyem hramye sem, i s'vyeroyu, blagogovyeniem i strahom Bozhiyim vhodyaschih von, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For our Most Holy Patriarch _______, and for our Bishop _______, for the venerable priesthood, for the diaconate in Christ, for all the clergy and the people, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O Svyatveyshem Patriyarsye nashem _______, i o Episkopye nashem _______, chestnyem presviterstvye, vo Hristye diakonstvye, o vsem pritchtye i lyudeh, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For the honorable and God-protected president of the United States, for our government and military forces, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O chestnyem i Bogohranimon prezidentya i pravitelystvye strani seya i vsem voinstvye nashem, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
To enable them to subdue every enemy and adversary, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O psobiti i pokoriti pod nozye ih vsyakago vraga i supostata, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For this city, for every city and countryside, and for them that with faith dwell in them, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O gradye sem, vsyakom gradye, stranye, i vyeroyu zhivushchih v'nih, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For seasonable weather, for abundance of the fruits of the earth, and for peaceful times, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O blagorastvoreniyi vozduhov, o izobiliyi plodov zemnih i vremenyeh mirnih, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For travelers by water, by land, and by air; for the sick and the suffering; for captives and their salvation, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O plavayusshchih, puteshestvuyushchih, i neduguyushchih, strazhdushchih, plyenennih, i o spaseniyi ih, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
For our deliverance from all tribulation, wrath, and want, let us pray to the Lord.
items.cu: O izbavitisya nam ot vsyakiya skorbi, gnyeva, i nuzhdi, Gospodu pomolimsya.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.
Protect us, save us, have mercy on us, and safeguard us, O God, by Thy Grace.
items.cu: Zastupi, spasi, pomiluy i sohrani nas, Bozhe, Tvoyeyu blagodatiyu.

items.en: Lord, have mercy.

+ Commemorating our most holy, most pure, and most blessed and glorious lady, the mother of God and ever-virgin Mary, with all the saints, let us commend ourselves and one another, and our whole life, unto Christ our God.
items.cu: + Presvyatuyu, prechistuyu, preblagoslovennuyu, slavnuyu Vladichitsu nashu Bogoroditsu i prisnodyevu Mariyu, so vsyemi svyatimi pomyanuvshe, sami sebe, i drug druga, i ves zhivot nash Hristu Bogu predadim.

items.en: To Thee, O Lord.
The Priest quietly says the following prayer, the Prayer of the First Antiphon:

O Lord our God, Whose power is ineffable, and Whose glory is incomprehensible, Whose mercy is infinite, and Whose love for mankind is ineffable: Do thou thyself, O Master, according to Thy tender compassion, look upon us, and upon this holy temple, and deal with us, and them that pray with us, according to Thine abundant mercies and compassions.
+ For unto Thee are due all glory, honor, and worship, to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever, and unto ages of ages.
items.cu: + Yako podobaet Tebye vsyakaya slava, chest i poklonenie, Otsu, i Sinu, i Svyatomu Duhu, ninye i prisno i vo vyeki vyekov.

items.en: Amen.
